methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $256K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terran Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zach Dwiel and Danny Weddle that has raised $256K to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), though peer valuation data at the Seed stage remains limited within this narrow segment.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $256K total raised as a proxy for current valuation, yielding $640K. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the robotics sector meeting minimum threshold criteria (three or more datapoints). The private peer universe includes companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, but these lack sufficient granular valuation detail at comparable stages to anchor a secondary method.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $544K (low) to $736K (high), with a median of $640K. This range reflects conservative positioning given reliance on a single methodological input and the inherent uncertainty around early-stage robotics valuations where comparable trading multiples and funding rounds remain sparse. Key risks include the absence of recent Seed-stage peer transactions and sector-specific factors affecting capital efficiency in hardware-heavy robotics development.
